final.hypothesis_statement: A precursor-based small-molecule inhibitor selectively passivates the non-growth surface while surface grows the target film on the growth surface, achieving >=90% selectivity at 10 nm for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

plan.method: AS-ALD differential-reactivity protocol (ADR-009): inhibitor adsorption screen on a-SiN (NGS) vs a-SiO2 (GS) over a gated surface ensemble, blocking coverage -> nucleation delay -> S(N).
plan.rationale: 'acetic acid' (carboxylic acid) is predicted to chemisorb on the NGS and physisorb on the GS, giving a large differential blocking coverage.
reasoning_trace: Retrieved 7 inhibitor candidates grounded in the KG (criteria: selection_criteria.md).
Ranked by differential adsorption + volatility + removability; selected 'acetic acid' (rank 1).
Paired with precursor 'BDEAS' for target film SiOx.

success_criteria.description: NGS is blocked substantially more than GS

assumptions: Adsorption-energy priors are literature/xTB values; Tier-1 MLIP recomputes them within a single calculator/head/dtype.
Only chemisorbed, purge-surviving inhibitor blocks the precursor.
Selectivity is reported as mean +/- std over the surface ensemble.

final.narrative: Tested 'acetic acid' inhibitor / 'BDEAS' precursor over 5 a-SiN (NGS) and 5 a-SiO2 (GS) gated surfaces using tier0-literature-priors. Differential blocking 0.945; S = 0.923 +/- 0.000 at 10.0 nm vs target 0.90 -> supported.

history.narrative: Tested 'acetic acid' inhibitor / 'BDEAS' precursor over 5 a-SiN (NGS) and 5 a-SiO2 (GS) gated surfaces using tier0-literature-priors. Differential blocking 0.945; S = 0.923 +/- 0.000 at 10.0 nm vs target 0.90 -> supported.

reflections.critique: Verdict 'supported' at confidence 0.56 is decisive; accepting.
